title: Iridodictyum pamphylicum
intro: Iridodictyum pamphylicum, also known as the Pamphylian iris, is a flowering plant species within the genus Iridodictyum. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of various ailments such as digestive issues and skin irritations. The bioactive constituents of Iridodictyum pamphylicum include iridoid glycosides, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Iridodictyum pamphylicum is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat digestive issues and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Iridodictyum pamphylicum is a rare orchid species, and its classification is as follows. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. Further classification places it within the subfamily Orchidoideae and the tribe Orchideae. This species is also part of the genus Iridodictyum, which was previously considered a synonym of Ophrys. However, recent studies have confirmed its distinctiveness within the orchid family.
